fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Florida Pharmacy Technician License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Florida - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacy technician license reciprocity florida, how to transfer pharmacy technician license to florida, pharmacy technician compact florida, florida pharmacy technician endorsement application

board.name: Florida Board of Pharmacy
board.parent_dept: Florida Department of Health, Board of Medicine
board.mailing_address: Department of Health, Board of Pharmacy, 4052 Bald Cypress Way, Bin C04, Tallahassee, FL 32399-3254

quick_facts.fee_breakdown: $50 application fee + $50 licensure fee + $5 unlicensed activity fee

license_types.title: Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacy technician in Florida.

steps.title: Create an Account on FL Health Source
steps.description: Register for an account on the Florida Department of Health's online licensing portal, FL Health Source (www.flhealthsource.gov), if you do not already have one. You will need a valid email address to complete registration.

steps.title: Submit Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your FL Health Source account, navigate to the 'Apply for a License' section, and select 'Pharmacy Technician'. Complete all required sections of the online application form with accurate personal, educational, and employment information.

steps.title: Complete Criminal History Background Check
steps.description: Initiate and complete the required criminal history background check by submitting your fingerprints via a LiveScan provider. Instructions and the LiveScan form are typically provided within the online application process or can be found on the FL DOH website.

steps.title: Upload Supporting Documentation and Pay Fee
steps.description: Upload all necessary supporting documents, such as proof of high school diploma or GED, directly to your application in the FL Health Source portal. Pay the non-refundable $55.00 application fee through the online system.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly log in to your FL Health Source account to monitor the status of your application. The Board of Pharmacy will communicate any requests for additional information or clarification through the portal.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

provisional_text: Florida offers a temporary or trainee pharmacy technician permit that allows new applicants to begin working in a pharmacy setting under direct pharmacist supervision while completing their certification requirements. Contact the Florida Board of Pharmacy for specific temporary permit restrictions and duration.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in Florida by submitting a new application to the Florida Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any Florida-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: How much does a Florida pharmacy technician license cost?
faqs.answer: The Florida Board of Pharmacy lists a total initial registered pharmacy technician fee of $105: $50 application, $50 licensure, and a $5 unlicensed activity fee. The biennial renewal fee is $55.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Florida have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Florida. To work in Florida, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Florida?
faqs.answer: The Florida Board of Pharmacy says to allow 30 days for initial review of a registered pharmacy technician application.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Florida while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: Contact the Florida Board of Pharmacy to determine whether a temporary or trainee permit is available while your endorsement application is being processed. Requirements vary, and some applicants may need to wait for full approval before beginning work in Florida.

reciprocity.endorsement_url: https://www.flhealthsource.gov/ (Select 'Apply for a License' and choose the Pharmacy Technician endorsement option once logged in. Details for endorsement requirements can be found on the Board's 'Applications and Forms' page).
reciprocity.notes: Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in Florida by submitting a new application to the Florida Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any Florida-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 4 to 6 Weeks.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacy Technician in Florida
hero.dek: Apply for your Florida Pharmacy Technician license online through the Florida Department of Health's FL Health Source Online Services Portal (www.flhealthsource.gov). Create an account, select the Pharmacy Technician application, and submit all required documentation.
